_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, districts 62-67 (state tier). Karen Hamilton (HD62 R), Phillip Scott (HD63 R), Stacey Carroll (HD64 D), Nicole Cole (HD66 D), Hillary Pugh Kent (HD67 R). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll-call records (LIS/VPAP), the 2026 constitutional-amendment votes (HJ1 repro, HJ3 marriage, HJ2 felon-voting), iVoterGuide/Virginia Voter Guide questionnaires, issue-group endorsements (REPRO Rising, EMILY's List, PP, NRA-PVF, VCDL, Everytown), and campaign/official sites. All 5 agents independently found NO cross-party deviations: the 3 R who voted for the marriage amendment were Bloxom/McNamara/Garrett (none in this batch). Republicans here are consistent conservatives; Democrats (Carroll/Cole) have uniformly progressive records. Carroll's individual votes were confirmed via the LIS member-vote API (HARD); Cole's individual amendment rows were NOT retrievable, so she is scored only on confirmed endorsements + stated positions + her documented Spotsylvania school-board record, not on party-bloc inference. Several genuinely thin dimensions left null per evidence-first discipline.

profile.religion: Christian (parents served as missionaries; specific congregation not publicly named)
profile.background: VA Delegate HD-62 (Greene, Madison, parts of Orange & Culpeper — central VA, NOT Fredericksburg); freshman, sworn in Jan 14, 2026 after defeating Democrat Sara Ratcliffe (Nov 2025). Structural/civil engineer (B.S. UNC Charlotte 2007), small-business owner in Orange. Homeschooling mother of three; husband Cameron Hamilton (former Navy SEAL). Orange County Moms for America leader.

karen-hamilton.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Freshman R, first (2026) session, so the documented legislative record is short; scored cells rest on her first-session amendment vote + explicit campaign positions, not party inference. HARD pro-life: campaign explicitly affirms life begins at conception and opposes VA becoming an 'abortion sanctuary'; spoke at the Richmond March for Life against the 2026 reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1, passed House 64-34 with every voting R opposed). On HJ3 (marriage amendment, House 67-31) the only 3 R yes-votes were Bloxom/McNamara/Garrett — she was NOT among them (voted no or absent; individual row behind JS-only LIS page). Campaign supports school choice, 'Law and Order' (endorsed by AG Miyares; seated on House Public Safety), and 2A repeal of existing gun-control laws (endorsed by Nick Freitas). Election integrity, federal overreach, border, economic-specifics, christian-liberty specifics: no documented position found -> null (not inferred from party).

0.note: Campaign explicitly states life begins at conception and lists 'The Right to Life' as a top priority; spoke at the March for Life against the 2026 reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1), which every voting Republican opposed.

1.note: Did NOT vote for HJ3, the amendment repealing VA's same-sex-marriage ban (House 67-31); the only 3 R yes-votes were Bloxom, McNamara, Garrett — she was not among them. Individual NO-vs-absent row not retrievable (LIS JS-only), but documented she did not support codifying same-sex marriage.

0.note: Campaign explicitly supports school choice ('create competition in education'); herself a homeschooling mother.
'Law and Order' campaign priority backing law enforcement; endorsed by AG Jason Miyares; seated on the House Public Safety committee.

1.note: Campaign 'Right to Self Defense' opposes new gun restrictions and seeks to repeal existing gun-control laws; endorsed by 2A advocate Nick Freitas.

profile.religion: Christian; raised in church, served as youth leader, assistant church planter, small-group teacher (current congregation not named); Liberty University triple-graduate (B.S. Religion, MA Mgmt/Leadership, Juris Master)
profile.background: VA Delegate HD-63 (Spotsylvania/Fredericksburg/Stafford); incumbent since 2022 (old 88th, redistricted to 63rd in 2024), re-elected Nov 2025, term ends Jan 2028. Chairs the House Rules Committee. Background investigator at CACI. Wife Elisabeth, five daughters.

phillip-scott.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Incumbent R with a multi-year record; chairs House Rules. iVoterGuide rated 'Verified Conservative' (2025). HARD: voted NO on HJ1 (2026 reproductive-freedom amendment, present in the GOP no-bloc) and NO on HJ3 (marriage amendment; not among the 3 R crossovers) and NO on HJ2 (felon-voting amendment; only R yes was Cherry). 2023: proposed reducing VA early voting from 45 to 14 days. 2A: endorsed by NRA-PVF (2025) and funded by Commonwealth Sportsmen's Alliance. Patroned HB1229 (school seclusion/restraint reporting -> law) and a 2022 vehicle-tax-reduction bill; Club for Growth Foundation Fellow. He declined most iVoterGuide questionnaire items, so several specifics (school choice, immigration, police, religious liberty) have no documented position -> null. q4 sanctity left null (no donor proof of abortion-industry money either way).

0.note: Voted NO on HJ1 (2026), the constitutional amendment establishing a fundamental right to reproductive freedom (House 64-34, straight party line; every R opposed). iVoterGuide rated him Verified Conservative.

1.note: Voted NO on HJ3 (House 67-31), the amendment repealing VA's same-sex-marriage ban; only 3 R (Bloxom/McNamara/Garrett) crossed over — he was not among them.

2.note: In the 2023 session proposed reducing Virginia's early-voting period from 45 to 14 days — favoring tighter, single-day-oriented in-person voting.

1.note: Endorsed by the NRA-PVF (2025) and funded by the Commonwealth Sportsmen's Alliance gun-rights PAC — documented opposition to new gun restrictions.

profile.religion: Member of Mount Zion Baptist Church (Triangle, VA)
profile.background: VA Delegate HD-64 (Stafford County); freshman, sworn in Jan 14, 2026 after defeating Republican incumbent Paul Milde (Nov 2025). CPA (VA & DC), master's in finance, federal-financial-statements auditor. ~19-20 yr Army/WV National Guard veteran (retired 2025); foster mother to 22 teens; mother of three.

stacey-carroll.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Freshman D; individual 2026 floor votes confirmed via the LIS member-vote API (member H0388) -> HARD roll-call evidence. Uniformly progressive scorecard record with no conservative deviation. Voted YES on HJ1 (repro amendment) and the companion repro bills; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, EMILY's List, and REPRO Rising. Voted YES on HJ3 (marriage amendment) and companion marriage bills. Voted YES on the full 2026 gun-control package (HB217/SB749 assault-firearm & magazine ban, HB1525 firearm restrictions, red-flag expansion). Voted YES on HB1441 (sanctuary-leaning bill restricting local-federal 287(g) immigration cooperation). On elections, voted to kill a photo-ID bill (HB1131) and for expanded absentee/early/Sunday voting. Voted YES on HB1499 (narrowing parental notification on sexually explicit instructional material / barring its use to remove library books). Her bipartisan votes were 90+ consensus items (city charters, behavioral-crisis LE agreements), not scorecard-direction breaks. Public-justice cells left null: her LE record is consensus bills + Democratic police-oversight bills (HB1476, HB1482), no affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar and no defund vote either.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1 (2026), the amendment enshrining a fundamental right to reproductive freedom/abortion in the VA Constitution (House 64-34).

1.note: Voted YES on companion reproductive-freedom measures (HB781, SB449, SJ1) — the opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia, EMILY's List, and REPRO Rising VA.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ3 (House 67-31), the amendment replacing VA's 'one man, one woman' constitutional definition with 'two adult persons' — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted YES on HJ3 and companion marriage bills (HB612, SJ3, SB311) to codify same-sex marriage in the VA Constitution.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1499/SB19, which narrow the parental-notification law on sexually explicit instructional material (excluding library books and barring its use to remove/censor books) — weakening conservative content review.

1.note: Voted to lay on the table (kill) HB1131, a voter photo-ID requirement bill, in subcommittee.

3.note: Voted YES on the Democratic elections package expanding absentee/early/Sunday voting and absentee-ballot cure/receipt deadlines (SB438, HB773/774, HB82) — opposite of restricting mass mail-in voting.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1441/SB783, a sanctuary-leaning law restricting state/local law enforcement from entering federal immigration-enforcement (287(g)-type) agreements.

1.note: Voted YES on the full 2026 gun-control package: HB217/SB749 (assault-firearm & magazine ban), HB1525/SB643 (firearm possession/purchase restrictions), and substantial-risk/'red flag' order expansion.

profile.religion: Not publicly confirmed (no specific congregation/denomination located)
profile.background: VA Delegate HD-66 (parts of Spotsylvania & Caroline); freshman, sworn in Jan 14, 2026 after defeating 35-yr Republican incumbent Bobby Orrock (Nov 2025). Financial advisor / small-business owner (MBA Finance, Northwestern Kellogg); founder of Pathways to Success Foundation. Former Spotsylvania County School Board member (2022-2026), board vice chair 2024. Mother of three.

nicole-cole.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Freshman D. Her individual 2026 floor roll-call rows were NOT retrievable (LIS/VPAP JS-only), so she is NOT scored on the first-day amendment votes by party inference; instead scored only on confirmed endorsements, documented stated positions, and her HARD Spotsylvania School Board record. Listed on REPRO Rising VA's endorsed-candidate page and publicly supports the reproductive-freedom amendment. As a school-board member she led the post-2023 majority that REVERSED the prior conservative board's removal of 'sexually explicit' library books (returned them to shelves; moved book-appropriateness decisions to the superintendent) and co-founded a PAC to defeat the book-restriction majority — a documented record against the conservative content-review/parental-flagging position. Opposes school vouchers. Received a $2,000 contribution from Everytown for Gun Safety Action Fund (VPAP) and campaigned on gun safety. Marriage-amendment vote, election integrity, public justice, federal overreach, border, christian-liberty: no documented individual position -> null (not inferred from party).

0.note: Publicly supports the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment and frames abortion access as a family/economic right — does not affirm personhood from conception.

4.note: Listed on REPRO Rising Virginia's endorsed-candidate/team page (abortion-rights advocacy organization).

0.note: Publicly opposes school vouchers; platform centers on public-school funding rather than school choice.

2.note: As a Spotsylvania School Board member, led the majority that reversed the removal of 'sexually explicit' library books and delegated book-appropriateness decisions to administrators — opposing conservative content review of explicit school materials.

1.note: Campaigned on gun safety and received a $2,000 contribution from the Everytown for Gun Safety Action Fund (Oct 2025) — documented alignment with the gun-control side. (Based on funding + public stance, not a roll-call vote.)

profile.religion: Lifelong member of Cobham Park Baptist Church, where she serves as a youth group leader
profile.background: VA Delegate HD-67 (Northern Neck: King George, Lancaster, Northumberland, Richmond, Westmoreland, part of Caroline); incumbent since Jan 2024, re-elected Nov 2025. Businesswoman (VP of family beverage distributor Al Pugh Distributing); Randolph-Macon College + MPA from VCU. Co-founded the Warsaw-Richmond County Main Street Program. Wife and mother of three boys.

hillary-pugh-kent.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Incumbent R with a 2024-2026 record + a documented Virginia Voter Guide questionnaire. HARD/documented: voted NO (GOP bloc) on HJ1 (2026 repro amendment; every R opposed) and on HJ3 (marriage amendment; not among the 3 R crossovers); Voter Guide records her opposing the unrestricted abortion amendment, supporting Born-Alive protection, opposing repeal of parental consent for minors' abortions, opposing the same-sex-marriage amendment, supporting a ban on minor gender-reassignment treatments, and supporting protection of female sports. Supports more education options (school choice) and opposes sexually explicit materials in public schools (Voter Guide). Public justice: campaign priority of 'funding law enforcement' + patroned HB1592 extending State Police retirement benefits to conservation police officers (affirmative pro-LE sponsorship). 2A: VCDL PAC endorsed (its endorsed House candidates 'earned a VERY PRO-GUN grade'). Election integrity, federal overreach, border, economic-specifics: thin/no documented record -> null. q4 sanctity (PP money) left null (no donor data).

0.note: Voted NO (with the unanimous R bloc) on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1); Virginia Voter Guide records her opposing the unrestricted abortion amendment, supporting Born-Alive Infant Protection, and opposing repeal of parental consent for minors' abortions.

1.note: Voted NO on HJ3 (marriage amendment; not among the 3 R crossovers) and the Virginia Voter Guide records her opposing the same-sex-marriage amendment.

2.note: Virginia Voter Guide: supports prohibiting gender-reassignment treatments for minors and protecting female sports — affirms biological sex / rejects transgender ideology in policy.

0.note: Virginia Voter Guide: supports more education options for families (school choice).

2.note: Virginia Voter Guide: opposes keeping sexually explicit materials in public schools.

0.note: Campaign priority of 'funding law enforcement'; patroned HB1592 (2025) extending State Police Officers' Retirement System benefits to DWR conservation police officers — affirmative pro-law-enforcement sponsorship.

1.note: Endorsed by the VCDL PAC (which states its endorsed House candidates 'have all earned a VERY PRO-GUN grade'); campaign priority of defending Second Amendment rights.
